description: No Return-Path header is present on an otherwise clean, trusted, all-passing message whose smtp.mailfrom aligns with From. returnPathDomain is null and returnPathNullReversePath is false (distinguishing a missing header from an intentional `<>`), so returnPathDomainMatchesFromDomain and envelopeSenderDomainsAgree are null and no Return-Path signal is emitted. Confirms a missing Return-Path stays silent rather than producing a noisy low-confidence signal.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com
